description_long: This object is a rugged, vintage-style shotgun with a wooden stock and fore-end, featuring a cylindrical barrel and a prominent tubular magazine beneath. The wood appears worn and stained, with visible grain and a slightly curved, broad buttstock for shoulder support. The receiver and barrel are metallic, showing a matte, dark finish with some signs of wear. A simple iron sight is mounted on top, and the trigger is housed within a small, rounded metal guard. The shotgun's overall design is utilitarian, with metal bands securing the magazine tube to the barrel. The object exudes a sense of durability and practicality, suitable for use in urban or rural environments.
description: A vintage-style shotgun with a worn wooden stock, tubular magazine, and matte metal barrel, designed for durability and practical use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, highlighting the wooden stock, metal barrel, and tubular magazine.
description_view_1: Rear angled view focusing on the broad, curved wooden buttstock and the alignment of the barrel and magazine.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wooden stock, metal receiver, and the length of the barrel and magazine tube.
description_view_3: Front angled view emphasizing the barrel opening, tubular magazine, and the alignment of the fore-end and stock.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel with a tubular magazine beneath; no ribbing or venting visible
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded, compact metal guard with no visible finger grooves or reinforcement
